maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 30, 2018 at 04:54

beinecke.thurman.xml converted for compliance with Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines with brbl-migrate-01.xsl (mr2007-08-13).

PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[WALLACE THURMAN COLLECTION ])//EN" "thurman.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).

did.unittitle: Wallace Thurman Collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Wallace Thurman Collection contains Correspondence, (including letters from Langston Hughes), Writings, primarily versions of Aunt Hagar's Children and the musical play Harlem, written with William Jourdan Rapp, and Personal Papers.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The Wallace Thurman Collection contains items presented to the James Weldon Johnson Collection after his death in 1934, many from Mrs. William Jourdan Rapp, the widow of Thurman's collaborator on "Harlem."

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Wallace Thurman Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Wallace Thurman Collection. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

bioghist.head: WALLACE THURMAN (1902-1934)
bioghist.p: Wallace Thurman was a novelist and playwright who found fame before his early death with the novel The Blacker the Berry and the play, "Harlem". A biographical sketch of Thurman written by Harold Jackman, can be found in folder 59 of this collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: NOTE: This finding aid was produced from a previously existing manuscripts inventory. All pertinent information has been retained.

did.unittitle: Correspondence
Letters
Grant, Helen from City Hospital
Harris, Robert
Hughes, Langston
Jackman, Harold
Jackman, Harold

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
letters from Jackman to Carl
Van Vechten re: Thurman
Collection

did.unittitle: Rapp, Paul
Rapp, William Jourdan
Rapp, Mrs. William Jourdan
Writings
Draft, typescript and carbon and printed version, corrected

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Includes: Table of Contents, Author's Preface,
"Notes on a Step-child" autobiographical
statement
"This Negro Literary Renaissance"
"Tribute"
"Mr. Van Vechten's Jurors"
"Frederick Douglass"
"Booker T. Washington"
"Marcus Garvey"
"Draw Your Own Conclusions"
"The Perpetual Bugaboo"
"The Coming Revolution"
"So This Is Harlem"
"Terpsichore in Harlem"

did.unittitle: "Harlem" (written with William Jourdan Rapp)
"Cordelia the Crude," scenario of "Harlem", typescript, corrected
"Cordelia the Crude," printed version, Fire!! A Quarterly Devoted to the Younger Negro Artists , Vol. 1, no. 1
"Color Parade", title pages and synopsis of early version of "Harlem", holograph and typescript carbon
Untitled, early versions of "Harlem", holograph, typescript, and carbon, corrected
"City of Refuge", early version of "Harlem", holograph, typescript, and carbon, corrected
"Black Mecca", early version of "Harlem" typescript carbon
"Black Belt", early version of "Harlem", typescript carbon (3 copies)
Assorted revised pages of "Harlem"
[EMPTY FOLDERS]
Clippings re: "Harlem"
Playbills, theater magazines and advertisements re: "Harlem"
Contract for Crosby Gaige to produce "Black Mecca"
Articles about "Harlem" and life in Harlem
"Harlem", article about play by Thurman and Rapp, printed version, Detroit Free Press
"The Writing of 'Harlem', the Story of a Strange Collaboration" by W. J. Rapp, holograph
"'Harlem' as Educational Drama," by Thurman and Rapp, holograph and typescript of article
Articles about the production of "Harlem", holograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Includes:"Casting and Directing
Harlem
Untitled article by Wallace Thurman
"How 'Harlem' Got to Broadway" by
W. J. Rapp
"My Collaboration" by Wallace Thurman

did.unittitle: "Two Playwrights Look at Harlem" by W. J. Rapp, holograph and typescript carbon
"What's Happening in the Black Belt", by Thurman and Rapp, holograph, typescripts and carbon, corrected, and printed version
"Few Know Real Harlem, the City of Surprises" by Thurman and Rapp, typescripts, corrected and printed version
"Harlem House-Rent Parties", one version by Wallace Thurman, one version by W. J. Rapp, holograph
"Sweetbacks" by W. J. Rapp, holograph
Untitled article re: dance called "The Bump," holograph
"Harlemese" and "Glossary of Harlemisms" by Wallace Thurman and W. J. Rapp, holographs
"A Lady: Past Revolution, in Memoriam: Circa 1936", poem, holograph
"Negro Artists and the Negro," article, printed version, The New Republic "
"Nephews of Uncle Remus", article, printed version, The Independent
Review of Langston Hughes; Not Without Laughter , printed version, New York Evening Post
Untitled autobiographical statement, holograph
Untitled poem fragment
Untitled, four pages of handwritten manuscript about tubercular patients
Personal Papers
Biographical sketches of Wallace Thurman by Harold Jackman and Hughes Allison
Clippings, re: Wallace Thurman
Untitled autobiographical statements by William Jourdan Rapp
William Jourdan Rapp, obituary and memorial address clippings
Photographs: Wallace Thurman, William Jourdan Rapp, and Chester Erskin
Restricted Fragile Papers

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 4: Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

Box 1, folder 19
